title: pygeum parvifolium
intro: Pygeum parvifolium, also known as African cherry or Pygeum, is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the Prunus genus. The plant's bark and seeds have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, primarily for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of prostate issues and urinary problems. The health benefits associated with Pygeum parvifolium include relief from prostate inflammation, improved urinary flow, and potential reduction in the risk of prostate cancer. Herbal preparations of Pygeum parvifolium typically involve the extraction of its bark and seeds, which can be found in capsule, tablet, or tincture form.
